input.description: Fix L2/L5/L6 safety blocks per Sol
input.prompt:
The integrated Sol max-reasoning gate FAILED the six-lever set with two SAFETY BLOCKS. Fix them in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff on branch fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate. Sol's full verdict is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/sol_integrated_fail_verdict.txt — read it first. All-off byte-identity PASSED and the verifier engine is untouched — keep both. Implement the three fixes below, add tests that reproduce Sol's failures then prove them fixed, run the suite. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run RACE.

FIX 1 (SAFETY — L2 table can fabricate). At multi_section_generator.py ~8072 (the synthesis-matrix validator, `_extract_synthesis_matrix`/its row validator): today it only checks a row's `[N]` exists somewhere in the section and optionally that decimals exist somewhere. Sol reproduced acceptance of invented institutes/contexts/measures/designs and numbers attached to the WRONG `[N]`. FIX: parse exactly the six cells (Study/Context/Measure/Finding/Design/Ref); for each row, require every non-"—" cell to be deterministically grounded in the SENTENCE(S) that carry that row's SAME `[N]` marker — i.e. locate the prose sentence(s) whose citation set includes the row's [N], and require each cell's salient tokens/numbers to appear in that sentence (not merely somewhere in the section). Any cell that cannot be grounded => DROP THE ROW (fail closed). If dropping rows takes the table below PG_SYNTHESIS_MATRIX_MIN_ROWS, suppress the table entirely. Never accept a cell value absent from the row's own cited sentence. Update the committed test at test_lever2_synthesis_matrix.py:42 which currently accepts study labels/designs absent from its prose — it must now require grounding.

FIX 2 (SAFETY — L2+L5 destroy tables). L5 (cross_section_repetition_guard) runs LATER (report assembly ~12577) than L2 (in _run_section), so L5 can replace the verified sentence a table row was built from, or absorb+delete a whole table (the splitter at verified_compose.py:215 breaks only before [A-Z0-9], so trailing "| Table..." rows get swallowed into a preceding unit and L5 replaces them). FIX (both):
  (a) ORDER: run L5 consolidation BEFORE L2 table attach so tables are built from post-consolidation prose and L5 never sees a table. If a full reorder is too invasive, the minimum is: L5 must skip/never mutate any unit containing a markdown table row or the synthesis-matrix block.
  (b) DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: make L5 preserve markdown table blocks byte-for-byte (never consume/replace a line containing a table pipe or the matrix header), and strengthen its guard to compare PER-SECTION marker multisets (not just the aggregate) AND assert no table block is lost. If any consolidation would drop a table block or change a per-section marker set, REVERT that consolidation (its existing revert path).
  Add a test reproducing Sol's two repros: (1) L5 replaces a sentence that supplied an L2 row -> now either L5 runs first so the row reflects final prose, or the row is preserved with its basis intact; (2) a table block is never deleted by L5 and the per-section guard catches it.

FIX 3 (SAFETY/faithfulness — L6 limitations not grounding-validated). At multi_section_generator.py ~8459 the reader-register limitations response gets only length/prefix checks, so the register rewrite could introduce an unsupported claim. PREFERRED FIX: make the L6 register translation DETERMINISTIC (a rule/template-based rewrite of the SAME telemetry facts into scholarly phrasing) with NO LLM generation surface — then there is nothing to fabricate and disclosure fidelity is provable. If you keep any generative path, it must validate the output's factual tokens against the telemetry source and fall back to a deterministic reader-register template on any mismatch. Same for the preamble register.

DO NOT touch L4 (its no-op-on-K3 wiring is a deferred BENEFIT item, not a safety issue — leave it). Do NOT weaken strict_verify/provenance/is_verified. Keep all flags default-off byte-identical.

TESTS: extend the existing lever test files (or add test_lever_integration_safety.py) with: L2 fabricated-cell -> row dropped/table suppressed (fail-closed); L2 number-on-wrong-[N] -> dropped; L2+L5 table-never-destroyed + per-section multiset guard; L6 deterministic register (no fabrication, telemetry facts preserved). Run: /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_lever1_render_blocks.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever2_synthesis_matrix.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever5_consolidation.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever6_register.py tests/polaris_graph/test_provenance_generator.py -x -q and iterate to green. Import smoke.

Return: files+lines changed, exactly how each cell is grounded to its row's cited sentence (fail-closed proof), how L5 now cannot destroy a table (ordering + guard), whether L6 is now deterministic, the new tests reproducing Sol's repros, pytest results, and anything still risky for the integrated Sol re-gate.

Run: /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_lever1_render_blocks.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever2_synthesis_matrix.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever5_consolidation.py tests/polaris_graph/test_lever6_register.py tests/polaris_graph/test_provenance_generator.py -x -q and iterate to green. attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
8023	def _extract_synthesis_matrix(
8024	    raw: str,
8025	    valid_citation_nums: set[int],
8026	    verified_prose: str = "",
8027	    min_rows: int = 3,
8028	) -> str:
8029	    """Extract + validate the `| Study | Context | ... |` synthesis matrix from an LLM
8030	    response. Returns the cleaned table (header + separator + data rows) or "" when it
8031	    should be SUPPRESSED. Mirrors ``_extract_trial_summary_table`` but domain-agnostic:
8032	
8033	      - canonical header + markdown separator required;
8034	      - every data row must cite at least one [N], and every [N] must be in
8035	        ``valid_citation_nums`` (the markers present in the verified prose) — rows with
8036	        an out-of-range/absent citation are DROPPED (reuse-only, never invent);
8037	      - when the cell-verify gate is on, every decimal in a row's cells must appear in
8038	        the verified prose (no fabricated/mis-transcribed numbers);
8039	      - the sentinel ``NO_COMPARABLE_STUDIES`` => "";
8040	      - fewer than ``min_rows`` surviving data rows => "" (suppress; a 1-2 row table is
8041	        worse than prose).
8042	    """
8043	    if not raw:
8044	        return ""
8045	    text = raw.strip()
8046	    if text == "NO_COMPARABLE_STUDIES":
8047	        return ""
8048	    text = re.sub(r"^```(?:markdown|md)?\s*", "", text)
8049	    text = re.sub(r"\s*```\s*$", "", text)
8050	
8051	    header_match = _SYNTHESIS_MATRIX_HEADER_RE.search(text)
8052	    if not header_match:
8053	        return ""
8054	    lines_after = text[header_match.start():].splitlines()
8055	    while lines_after and not lines_after[0].strip():
8056	        lines_after = lines_after[1:]
8057	    if len(lines_after) < 2:
8058	        return ""
8059	    header_line = lines_after[0].strip()
8060	    separator_line = lines_after[1].strip()
8061	    if not _MARKDOWN_TABLE_SEPARATOR_RE.match(separator_line):
8062	        return ""
8063	
8064	    _cell_verify = _table_cell_verify_enabled() and bool(verified_prose.strip())
8065	    _prose_decimals: set[str] = set()
8066	    if _cell_verify:
8067	        from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import (
8068	            _decimals as _sv_decimals,
8069	        )
8070	        _prose_decimals = _sv_decimals(_CITATION_MARKER_RE.sub("", verified_prose))
8071	
8072	    kept_rows: list[str] = []
8073	    for line in lines_after[2:]:
8074	        stripped = line.strip()
8075	        if not stripped:
8076	            break
8077	        if not stripped.startswith("|"):
8078	            break
8079	        nums = [int(m.group(1)) for m in _CITATION_MARKER_RE.finditer(stripped)]
8080	        if not nums:
8081	            continue  # rule 2: every row must cite
8082	        if any(n not in valid_citation_nums for n in nums):
8083	            continue  # out-of-range/invented citation => drop the row
8084	        if _cell_verify:
8085	            from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import (
8086	                _decimals as _sv_decimals,
8087	            )
8088	            _row_data = _CITATION_MARKER_RE.sub("", stripped)
8089	            if not _sv_decimals(_row_data).issubset(_prose_decimals):
8090	                continue  # a cell number not in the verified prose => fabricated => drop
8091	        kept_rows.append(stripped)
8092	
8093	    if len(kept_rows) < max(3, int(min_rows)):
8094	        return ""  # suppress: not enough comparable rows to be worth a table
8095	    return "\n".join([header_line, separator_line, *kept_rows])
8096	
8097	
8098	async def _call_synthesis_matrix(
8099	    *,
8100	    verified_prose: str,
8101	    model: str,
8102	    temperature: float,
8103	    max_tokens: int,
8104	) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
8105	    """Generate the cross-study synthesis matrix from a section's verified prose.
8106	
8107	    Returns (table_text, input_tokens, output_tokens). ``valid_citation_nums`` is
8108	    derived from the [N] markers PRESENT in ``verified_prose`` itself, so the table can
8109	    only reuse citations the prose already carries. Empty string when the prose lacks
8110	    3+ comparable studies (LLM returns ``NO_COMPARABLE_STUDIES``), the call fails, or no
8111	    valid table survives validation. No fabrication surface: the input prose is already
8112	    strict-verified and the table is APPENDED (prose is never altered).
8113	    """
8114	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
8115	        OpenRouterClient,
8116	        set_reasoning_call_context,
8117	    )

8119	    if not verified_prose or not verified_prose.strip():
8120	        return "", 0, 0
8121	    valid_nums = {int(m.group(1)) for m in _CITATION_MARKER_RE.finditer(verified_prose)}
8122	    if len(valid_nums) < 3:

content.text: The current validator checks a row's `[N]` is in-range and (optionally) decimals exist *somewhere* — but never binds a cell to the sentence carrying that row's citation. That's the fabrication hole. Let me see the header regex and citation/number helpers, then implement per-row grounding:

echo "=== header regex + citation marker regex ==="
sed -n '7978,7982p' src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
grep -n '_CITATION_MARKER_RE = \|_MARKDOWN_TABLE_SEPARATOR_RE = ' src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py | head
echo "=== is split_into_sentences importable here? ==="
grep -n 'from .provenance_generator import\|split_into_sentences' src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py | head -3
echo "=== _decimals signature (number extraction) ==="
grep -n 'def _decimals' src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py

    r"^\s*\|\s*Study\s*\|\s*Context\s*\|\s*Measure\s*\|\s*Finding"
    r"\s*\|\s*Design\s*\|\s*Ref\s*\|\s*$",
    re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,
)

content.text: `split_into_sentences` is already imported and `_CITATION_MARKER_RE` captures the number. Now let me implement **Fix 1** — per-row grounding that binds every cell to the sentence carrying that row's `[N]`, fail-closed. Inserting the grounding helpers before `_extract_synthesis_matrix`:

content.content: The file /home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +    "the a an of to in on for and or by with from at as is are was were be been being "
+    "that this these those it its their there here than then also more most less into "
+    "over under about across between among per via not no but which who whom whose "
+    "have has had will would can could may might such both each any all some".split()
+    """Lowercase + collapse every non-alphanumeric run to a single space (for substring grounding)."""
+    """The bare number tokens (integer/decimal) in a string — the values a cell asserts."""
+    """Map each citation number [N] to the concatenated text of the verified-prose SENTENCES that
+    carry it, so a table row citing [N] can be grounded against the exact sentence(s) it claims to
+    summarize — NOT merely against 'somewhere in the section' (the fabrication hole Sol found)."""
+    return {n: " ".join(v) for n, v in acc.items()}
+def _synthesis_row_grounded(cells: list[str], nums: list[int], sent_by_num: dict[int, str]) -> bool:
+    """FAIL-CLOSED grounding: every non-blank cell (except the Ref column) must be supported by the
